title: Spotlight | NYU Shanghai
description: Jointly established by New York University and East China Normal University, NYU Shanghai is the third degree-granting campus in NYU global network, joining NYU Abu Dhabi and NYU New York City.
text:
Spotlight
Spring Shows at NYU ShanghaiAs the semester drew to a close, our dance, arts, and IMB/IMA students shared their final projects.
Students Bring Events and Projects on Identity, Feminism, and Labor Rights to CampusStudent are raising awareness and educating our community on issues and matters close to their hearts.
China Bans For-Profit After-School Tutoring To Help Ease Academic Pressure on Kids -- Will It Work?NYU Shanghai’s Sociology, Business, and Mental Health experts offer their takes on the ban.
Yes, all this screen time is hurting your eyesNYU Shanghai Chief Scientist Lu Zhong-Lin looks into eye damage under quarantine.
How Can Digital Tools Solve the COVID Mental Health Crisis?Global public health researchers investigate the promise of digital interventions across Asia and Oceania.
Masks Off (if you wish)! Following other Shanghai universities, NYU Shanghai shifts to a new 'masks optional' mode.
“Workers in the Place of Soldiers”: the Forgotten Story of China and the Great WarThe Forgotten Story of China and the Great War.
What I’m Working on: Do Shanghai Parents Spend More on Their Kids When the School District They Live in Suddenly Improves? Do Shanghai Parents Spend More on Their Kids When the School District They Live in Suddenly Improves?
Celebrating International Women’s Day 2021Members of the NYU Shanghai community tell us about—and show us—the women that inspire them.
Home for the HolidaysStudents and their families opened their Shanghai homes up to classmates unable to travel for CNY.
The Neighborhoods Behind Wuhan’s RecoveryA professor and her team uncover how neighborhoods help reduce mental distress during COVID-19.
The Post-COVID ClassroomHow has going virtual transformed live teaching at NYU Shanghai?
Course Spotlight: Recycling Materials While Serving Others Transforming recyclables into trolleys, bookshelves, and space dividers to improve life on campus.
Life Design: Prototyping Your Own FutureStudents use design thinking to "try on" careers and life paths.
What I’m Working On: Almaz Zelleke Explores the Possibilities of Universal Basic IncomeUBI isn't a brand new idea. Professor Zelleke studies its historical precedents and applications.
